answer:
In Allama Iqbal's poem "Visal" (meaning "union") from *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, the speaker—metaphorically the nightingale (representing the longing soul)—celebrates achieving union with the beloved, depicted as the long-sought flower. This mystical union ends prior torment, transforms suffering into ecstasy, and reveals true liberty through surrender.[^1]

The poem opens with the joy of attainment:  
> The flower whose search, O Nightingale, would make me pine,  
> By blessed fate, that very flower is finally mine.[^2]

It reflects on the preceding anguish of separation—restless impatience, failure amid flowers, inner chaos—and contrasts it with the soul's renewal: turmoil vanishes, passion's blisters become flames, dark dust turns to a bright mirror reflecting the beloved's light.[^3] Union is paradoxical, granting freedom via bondage:  
> By entering chains, I found my liberty,  
> My home was built upon the wreck of me.[^4]

The beloved's glance ignites the heart, teaching artful surrender on this "blessed day."[^5] Overall, "Visal" portrays union as spiritual apotheosis, where ego dissolves into divine love's radiance, aligning with Iqbal's Sufi-inspired themes of self-realization.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, "Visal," [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, "Visal," [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-cade15).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, "Visal," paragraphs [p-553122](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-553122), [p-ea63e5](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-ea63e5), and [p-3a1ddc](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-3a1ddc).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, "Visal," [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-456772).

[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, "Visal," [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/27-visal/translation/#p-418f98).
